description: Letter from Isma’il b. Barhun al-Taharti to Efrayim b. Shemarya (fl. 1007–1055), Fustat. After a disagreement over selling indigo (it is not clear what the disagreement was about). The writer informs Efrayim, the judge and leader of the Jews in Fustat that Rav Hayya Gaon (r. 998–1038 CE) sent his answer and he supports the Tahartis side. The writer writes his Hebrew name – Shemuel b. Rabbi Avraham. Dating: ca. 1007–1038 CE, based on the dates when Efrayim b. Shemarya and Rav Hayya Gaon– though this range might be narrowed further based on the years when Isma’il b. Barhun al-Taharti was active. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 2, #124) VMR
description_he: מכתב מישמעאל בן ברהון אל-טאהרתי לאפרים בן שמריה, פוסטאט, כ-1007-1038, על תמיכת רב האי גאון בצד הטאהרתים בסכסוך אינדיגו
